updates.news:
Here's a summary of recent announcements, news, contracts, projects, and investments across quantum tech, drone tech, robotics, space tech, satellite communication, nuclear energy, AI, and latest innovative technologies, along with a table of relevant 2x leveraged ETFs.

## Government and Defense Announcements

**Quantum Technology**
The U.S. Department of Commerce announced $2.013 billion in federal incentives through the CHIPS and Science Act for nine quantum companies. IBM is slated to receive $1 billion to establish America's first pure-play quantum chip foundry, while GlobalFoundries will receive $375 million to scale its quantum computing hardware manufacturing. Other companies, including Quantinuum, Rigetti, D-Wave, and Infleqtion, are each expected to receive around $100 million in funding for advancements in various quantum computing systems.

**Drone Technology**
The Pentagon has initiated its "Drone Dominance" program by accepting small, one-way attack drones, with an order of 20,000 FPV (First-Person View) drones from ten different vendors, including Neros. The Army has also awarded Griffon Aerospace a $68 million contract to deliver Outlaw Gen 3 drones. Furthermore, the Pentagon's Lethality Prize Challenge, part of the Drone Dominance program, recognized Bravo Ordnance, Kela Technologies, Kraken Kinetics, Mountain Horse Solutions, and Northrop Grumman for developing cost-effective, mass-producible payloads for small drones. A new experimental U.S. military drone featuring hybrid-electric stealth technology successfully completed its inaugural flight. Smart Shooter secured a $3.4 million follow-on contract with the U.S. Marine Corps for fire control systems, with potential options increasing the total to $5.8 million.

**Robotics**
NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) is at the forefront of robotic exploration, developing technologies for missions to Mars, the Moon, asteroids, and icy moons. Key projects include the Limbed Excursion Mechanical Utility Robot (LEMUR), Axel Rover, DuAxel, A-PUFFER, and BRUIE. NASA is planning the Moonfall mission for 2028, which will deploy four robotic drones to the Moon's south pole for mapping, detecting underground water, and supporting the Artemis program in selecting human landing sites. Firefly Aerospace has been selected to construct the spacecraft for this mission. Additionally, NASA's Astrobee, a free-flying robotic system, is aiding astronauts on the International Space Station with routine tasks.

**Space Technology & Satellite Communication**
The U.S. Space Force awarded SpaceX a $2.29 billion contract (announced May 26, 2026) to develop a new satellite communications system, the Space Data Network Backbone, to ensure resilient data transport for the Joint Force. SpaceX also secured a separate $4.16 billion contract from the Space Force (announced May 29, 2026) under the Space-Based Airborne Moving Target Indicator (SB-AMTI) program, aimed at establishing a satellite constellation capable of tracking airborne threats globally. Northrop Grumman was awarded a $398 million contract by the Space Force to develop the Enhanced Protected Tactical Satellite Communications – Prototype (Enhanced PTS-P). SD Government, a Gogo company, obtained a multi-year blanket purchase agreement worth $7.5 million with the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to provide satellite communication services for "Hurricane Hunting" aircraft.

**Nuclear Energy**
The U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) announced the successful completion of a zero-power fueled criticality demonstration of Antares Nuclear's Mark-0 advanced reactor design at Idaho National Laboratory, as part of the DOE Reactor Pilot Program. The DOE has allocated $94 million to American companies to accelerate the deployment of small modular reactors. An initiative to bolster the nation's nuclear fuel cycle has been unveiled by the DOE's Defense Production Act Consortium. The DOE is seeking private sector partnerships for used nuclear fuel recycling and has awarded $5.9 million to universities for nuclear energy research. The world's first Microreactor Test Bed (DOME) is now operational at Idaho National Laboratory.

**Artificial Intelligence (AI)**
U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken announced the Global AI Research Agenda (GAIRA) and the AI in Global Development Playbook, outlining comprehensive approaches for AI research and its application in achieving UN Sustainable Development Goals. The Department of State also released its "Enterprise Artificial Intelligence Strategy FY 2024-2025." President Trump signed an Executive Order on June 2, 2026, to advance American AI innovation, focusing on cybersecurity and critical infrastructure protection. In May 2023, the National Science Foundation allocated $140 million to establish seven new National AI Research Institutes.

## Magnificent 7 Companies, Prominent Investors, and Industry Figures Investments

**Elon Musk (SpaceX/xAI)**
SpaceX is preparing for an initial public offering (IPO) with an estimated valuation of $1.75 trillion, a significant portion of which is attributed to its burgeoning AI business. The company intends to launch AI data centers into Earth orbit, with a dedicated factory in Bastrop, Texas, aiming for 1 gigawatt of space-based AI computing power by the end of 2027. xAI, a separate AI venture by Musk, merged with SpaceX in February 2026 and more than doubled its capital expenditure to $12.7 billion in 2025.

**Sam Altman (OpenAI, Helion Energy, Oklo, Merge Labs, Rain AI)**
Sam Altman, CEO of OpenAI, has engaged in discussions with senior Trump administration officials regarding the federal government potentially acquiring equity stakes in AI companies. OpenAI has confidentially filed for an IPO. Altman serves as Chairman of Helion Energy, a nuclear fusion company, and led a $500 million funding round in 2021. Microsoft entered into a power-purchase agreement with Helion in 2023. He also facilitated the public listing of Oklo, a nuclear fission company, through his SPAC in May 2024, later stepping down from the board in April 2025 to mitigate potential conflicts of interest. In January 2026, Altman co-founded Merge Labs, a brain-computer interface startup, which received $252 million from OpenAI. His investment portfolio also includes an early investment in Rain AI, an AI chip startup.

**Peter Thiel (Quantum-Systems, Stark Defence)**
Peter Thiel invested in Quantum-Systems GmbH, a German leader in drone robotics, in October 2022. Quantum-Systems subsequently raised €180 million in a Series C extension round in November 2025, increasing its valuation to €3 billion, with Thiel's Founders Fund among the investors. Thiel has also backed Stark Defence, a German defense startup specializing in kamikaze drones, which is reportedly close to securing a significant deal with the German Bundeswehr.

**Magnificent 7 Companies (General AI/Tech Investments)**
The U.S. Department of State launched the Partnership for Global Inclusivity on AI (PGIAI) in collaboration with major tech companies including Amazon, Apple, Google (Alphabet), IBM, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, and OpenAI, aimed at leveraging AI for sustainable development. Microsoft’s power-purchase agreement with Sam Altman-backed Helion Energy highlights a Magnificent 7 company's engagement in cutting-edge nuclear energy. Nvidia's computing platforms remain crucial for many AI initiatives, including OpenAI's training infrastructure and other ventures backed by Sam Altman.
